title: BHAG

content.statement: Set a Big Hairy Audacious Goal that galvanizes the team for 10 to 30 years.
content.question: What is the 10-year goal so audacious that pursuing it would change how you run the company?
content.mental_model: Summit visible from base camp, bold enough to organize all decisions toward it.
content.business_lens: Define a 10-to-30-year goal that excites and frightens the team in equal measure; break it into annual milestones.
content.life_lens: Pick a long-horizon goal that gives meaning to the small daily decisions.
sop: Use the goal as a filter for major decisions
content.explainer: NASA’s Apollo goal, land on the Moon by decade’s end, is the canonical BHAG. The goal organized thousands of sub-decisions and recruited talent that would not have shown up for incremental work. The risk is BHAGs without supporting discipline; pair with the 20-Mile March principle.
questions: What is the 10-year goal so audacious that pursuing it would change how you run the company?
Would this goal actually scare you a little if you said it out loud to your team?
What would you have to stop doing today if you took this goal seriously?

definitions.def: Big Hairy Audacious Goal, a bold, clear, long-range target (10 to 30 years) that focuses and energizes an organization.
Jim Collins's discipline of hitting the same steady, moderate target every period in good times and bad, instead of surging when it's easy and stalling when it's hard. Consistency beats bursts.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Audacity 9, Clarity 6, Belief 7. Score = 9 times 6 times 7 = 378 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means the goal organizes decisions and recruits talent on its own. Lower means it's just a big number nobody actually believes or knows how to reach.
content.closing_statement: A goal too safe won't organize anything.

caption:
Principle Nº 274: BHAG

Set a Big Hairy Audacious Goal that galvanizes the team for 10 to 30 years. NASA’s Apollo goal, land on the Moon by decade’s end, is the canonical BHAG.

Ask yourself: What is the 10-year goal so audacious that pursuing it would change how you run the company?
